claims.statement: +0x18da SEMANTICS: it is the PART-1 AIM PITCH short (BAM16) — element 1 of the stride-6 aim-record array based at +0x18d4. Writer: zz_006e1d0_ @0x8006e1d0 (via the wrapper zz_006e1ac_(actor, mask, part) = zz_006e1d0_(actor, mask, part, 0x4000)); with a lock target it computes desired = clamp(−(s16)actor+0x70 − FUN_800452a0(muzzleY_delta, horizDist), ±0x4000) from target pos +0x518 minus the per-part muzzle row (actor + part*0x30 + 0x8e0/0x8f0/0x900), steps toward it at page-turn-rate × (f32)actor+0x768 per tick, and returns 1 when reached / 0 stepping / −1 no-target (no-target path decays the record by FLOAT_804376d8 = 0.9). The engine turns this pitch into launch velocity: hSpeed(+0x44) = 40·cos(pitch), yVel(+0x48) = −40·sin(pitch), recomputed every tick in phases 2 and 3 (homing while the +0x1d10 window is open) and frozen at phase-3→4. FUN_800452a0 is un-decompiled (LIKELY atan2-shape from its (dy, dist) argument pair); the exact sign convention of the pitch is therefore LIKELY, but the formula and addresses are exact.

Host/actor hooks needed beyond the existing StreamContext: (1) aimPitch read+seek — part-1 record +0x18da with seek-toward-target clamp ±0x4000 at page-rate×dt and no-target 0.9 decay (phases 0/1/2, and 3 while tracking window open); (2) rangeCheck(prevActionIndex) using rows [1000,1000,2500] (identical for pl0400/pl040a/pl0406) × actor scale, driving the +0x541 lock-invalidate; (3) gravityFall from the movement page (−0.6 for all three borgs) written to gravityCoeff at P3→4; (4) landed() ground-snap boolean (zz_00677b0_ return) for P4; (5) yaw-only ground physics + dt-corrected decel 0.95 for P6 (already ported for the melee-lunge machine); (6) upper-cue latch 0x2c on landing (clears frozen bit 0x40, +0x87=0, +0x69c=0); (7) optional onEffect(subtype 0|2) FX hook; (8) exits: air-fall (zz_006a5a4_) on P4 timeout, ground-idle (zz_006a474_) at P6 — and NO attack-cooldown seed (+0x694 untouched, unlike zz_00ff2bc_). Registration: wire as actionTable[2] for family ctors 0x80074940 (pl0400, pl040a) and 0x801854f8 (pl0406); per-tick handler must ALSO apply cfg.aimRecover to +0x18e0/+0x18e2 before phase dispatch (that is the wrapper body).

claims.statement: GAMEPLAY SHAPE (synthesis for the orchestrator): this shared X is an AIMED AERIAL DIVE/LEAP SHOT machine, distinct from zz_00ff2bc_ (blink-melee) and zz_00fed6c_ (melee lunge). Sequence: P0 blink-away windup + stream A (air variant only changes header args); P1 hold up to 60f until facing target; P2 aim window — on the stream's contact byte, launch at 40 u/f along the part-1 aim pitch with zero decel/gravity; P3 20f minimum powered flight with stream-driven homing window (+0x1d10, canceled if hit via +0x1d9), then on the stream's +0x1b01 latch switch to stream B with the borg's own fall gravity (−0.6); P4 ballistic descend up to 60f — land (stream C + cue 0x2c + FX) or air-fall exit; P5/P6 landing skid with 0.95 decay, stream D, ground-idle exit below speed 5. Both member families are pl04xx gun-borg families (pl0400/pl040a and pl0406) whose other actions route to the same 0x800f2xxx-0x800f3xxx shared-shooter engine cluster (zz_00f1e30_/zz_00f2374_/zz_00f2764_/zz_00f3028_/zz_00f33f4_/zz_00f3738_), so the X machine reads like the shooter cluster's 'jump-jet dive attack'.
evidence: phase transcriptions in M3; family action tables in W1; cluster membership: chunk_0010.c FUN_80074d08→zz_00f1e30_(actor,&DAT_802d4ea8), FUN_80074ea8/ecc/ef0/f14→zz_00f2374_/zz_00f2764_/zz_00f3028_/zz_00f3738_; chunk_0046.c FUN_80185784/7a8/7cc/7f0→zz_00f2374_/zz_00f2764_/zz_00f33f4_/zz_00f3738_

findings.portImplications: Port this as a second parameterized shared machine alongside shared-x-special.ts (suggested packages/combat/src/families/shared-aerial-dive-x.ts). Key differences from the zz_00ff2bc_ port to respect: (1) the config is NOT a block — it is {slotBase: 0, aimRecover} where aimRecover is the wrapper's per-tick decay of the part-2 aim shorts (+0x18e0/+0x18e2): ×0.9 for pl0400/pl040a, ×0.5 (>>1) for pl0406; apply it in the factory-returned handler before phase dispatch, exactly where the ROM wrapper runs. (2) Four sequential group-4 stream slots (slotBase+0..3) instead of one, with the airborne fork changing only header args (10,2) — safe to ignore until decoded. (3) Launch velocity is DERIVED EVERY TICK from the part-1 aim pitch (+0x18da): hSpeed=40·cos(p), yVel=−40·sin(p), with a stream-gated homing window (+0x1d10, canceled by damage byte +0x1d9); the bridge needs an aimPitch field seeded by a seek-toward-target with ±0x4000 clamp (a lockPitch analog of the existing lockYaw). (4) Gravity is OFF during launch (gravityCoeff=0) and becomes the borg page's gravityFall (−0.6 for all three members) only at the P3→4 transition — reuse movementData's gravityFall export directly. (5) NO +0x694 cooldown seed at exit (do not copy shared-x-special's 4.0+dt), exits are plain zz_006a474_/zz_006a5a4_ analogs plus the cue-0x2c latch (clear frozen bit 0x40) on landing. (6) The phase-0 range check (rows 1000/1000/2500 × actor scale, indexed prevActionIndex%3, identical for all three borgs) only decides whether the lock is dropped (+0x541) — with no target the machine still runs, it just flies straight at current yaw/pitch; model it as lock invalidation, not a move gate. Registration: actionTable[2] for ctor families 0x80074940 (pl0400, pl040a) and 0x801854f8 (pl0406). P5's same-tick fall-through into P6 matters for frame parity (the skid stream starts and the decel/exit check still runs that tick). The +0x868 range-row writer open question from the ninja decode is now CLOSED (chunk_0007.c:31-38: page+0x134..+0x154 → actor+0x868..+0x888) — movementData.ts can export the three range rows for any future machine that needs them.
findings.summary: Engine 0x800f41c4 is a 7-phase shared AERIAL DIVE-SHOT X machine (phase table 0x8031b92c, phases 0x800f4214/4340/43f8/44e0/4610/4730/47a4) used by the pl04xx shooter families A (ctor 0x80074940, pl0400/pl040a, wrapper FUN_80074f38) and B (ctor 0x801854f8, pl0406, wrapper FUN_80185814). The cfg=None mystery is solved: the machine takes NO config block — its only parameter is r4 = group-4 stream slot base (both wrappers emit `li r4,0`, engine passes r4 untouched into phase 0 which stores it to the stream cursor +0x6ea), and the only other per-family code is the wrapper's aim-recovery decay of part-2 aim shorts +0x18e0/+0x18e2 (A: ×0.9, B: >>1). Machine shape: blink-away windup (motion=(pos−target)×0.95) + stream slot 0 → face target (≤60f) → aim window; on stream contact byte +0x1cef launch at 40 u/f along part-1 aim pitch +0x18da (hSpeed=40·cos, yVel=−40·sin, zero gravity) → ≥20f homing flight (window +0x1d10, damage byte +0x1d9 cancels), then stream slot 1 + gravityCoeff = page gravityFall (−0.6 all three borgs) → ballistic descend ≤60f → land (stream slot 2, cue 0x2c, FX) or air-fall exit zz_006a5a4_ → skid (slot 3, decel 0.95) → ground-idle exit zz_006a474_ below speed 5. No +0x694 cooldown. All constants DOL-read; wrapper→engine bl instructions, ctor→virtuals[0]→actionTable[2] chain, and per-borg page values (range rows 1000/1000/2500, turn rate 2560) fully derived; also closed the '+0x868 range-row writer' open question (chunk_0007.c:31-38 copies page+0x134..+0x154).

findings.portImplications: Port this as a SECOND parameterized shared machine alongside shared-x-special.ts — but with a crucial architectural difference the code comments must state: the ROM has NO per-family config block here (the scout's 'config 0x80325acc' is actually the global phase table). The TS config object is a port-side convenience derived from borgNumber, not a ROM structure. Concretely: (1) one createSeries3XSpecial(cfg, ctx) factory, wired as actionTable[2] AND actionTable[3] for all four families (pl0301; pl0302+pl0306; pl0303; pl0307+pl030d) — six borgs total, four ctors, one engine; (2) phase shapes are sharedXPhase0/1/2 minus the combo-cursor increment, with group 2 slots 0x10/0x11, face(0xc1) retained in ALL phases, and a groundSnap+pos-revert call each frame; (3) per-borg onHit map: 0x301 -> child(kind 2, sub 0x13); 0x302 -> blast(rec 0x1f, vel = speedVec*(5,5,5)); 0x303 -> ammoGate(slot 2, cost 1) ? blast(rec 0x3b) : commonExit-immediately (note: ammo failure CANCELS the move, a behavior shared-x has no analog for); 0x306 -> none; (4) 0x307/0x30d get the phase-2 repeating follow-up stream (slots 0x12/0x13) gated on contactP1>0 && ammoSlot2>0 — requires mirroring the +0x1cf0 contact byte and a raw ammo-count read into the bridge, both new host hooks; (5) two-tier cooldown (8.0 air-landing / 16.0 common exit) vs ninja's single 4.0 — RomActor needs no new fields for this, just the two constants; (6) the +0x1900/+0x18da meter decays are engine-visible but consumer-less in this dig — port them as tracked-but-unused actor scalars with an UNRESOLVED comment so the eventual consumer dig can light them up; (7) famB's companion spawn (zz_01d2ffc_, child kind 0x28) is OUTSIDE the phase machine and its trigger is untraced — do not wire it into the X phases; leave it as a documented residual like ninja's shuriken table host-wiring.
findings.summary: Transcription-grade dig of shared X-machine 0x8010ce60 complete, all raw-disasm verified. Headline architecture finding: this machine is GLOBAL and CONFIG-LESS — 0x80325acc is its 3-entry PHASE table ([0x8010ceb0, 0x8010cfbc, 0x8010d0d0, NULL]), not a config block, and 0x80325ab8 is a 5-entry variant table whose entries are ALL 0x8010ce60. 'Handler' 0x8010cdec = the action-table entry (decays meter +0x1900 x0.96 then dispatches variantTable[+0x581]); 'wrapper' 0x8010ce60 = the phase dispatcher (+0x541 target-invalidation prologue, then phaseTable[+0x540]). No bl call sites exist for either; families reference 0x8010cdec directly in their root action tables. Per-family behavior lives in INLINE BORG SWITCHES on actor+0x3e8: phase-1 on-hit payloads (0x301 -> zz_00c3be0_(0x13); 0x302 -> zz_0082824_(0x1f); 0x303 -> ammo-gated zz_0082824_(0x3b) with no-ammo -> immediate exit) and a phase-2 repeating follow-up stream (slots 0x12/0x13) for 0x307/0x30d gated on contact byte +0x1cf0 and ammo count +0x784. Machine shape: P0 blink-reposition (motion=(pos-target)*0.95, group-2 stream slot 0x10/0x11, rate -1.0), P1 tick+face+reposition+hit-check, P2 gravity-1.0 recovery with descriptor-gravity restore on +0x1d0f, dual cooldowns 8.0 (air-landing, cue 7 via zz_006a750_) / 16.0 (common exit via zz_006a474_/zz_006a5a4_). Bonus: the engine serves FOUR families / six borgs — two Ghidra-missed ctors 0x80106e3c (pl0301) and 0x80107e7c (pl0302+pl0306) found by raw scan, plus the task's 0x8019c380 (pl0303) and 0x801ba020 (pl0307+pl030d); all four root dispatchers, vtables, and action-table words dumped as wrapper proof. Every float DOL-read; +0x1d10 and +0x558/+0x560 confirmed absent.

claims.statement: Port shape mirroring shared-x-special.ts — new shared machine 'shared-flight-x.ts' (knight-line lance charge), 6 phases. Interface: interface SharedFlightXConfig { groundSlot: number /* cfg+0x00, group-4 slot when grounded */; airSlot: number /* cfg+0x04, when +0x5e0&0x40 */; landSlot: number | null /* cfg+0x08, −1→null; group-4 landing anim (unused by all 5 shipped configs) */; reposScale: number /* cfg+0x0c: 0.95 or 0.92 */; flightTime: number /* cfg+0x10 raw; runtime = ×10, −10 per active frame */; extendBudget: number /* cfg+0x12 raw; runtime = ×10 */; extendRate: number /* cfg+0x14 = 8 */; extendRateFloor: number /* cfg+0x16 = 2 */; speed0: number /* cfg+0x18 = 0 */; speedMax: number /* cfg+0x1c = 30 */; accel: number /* cfg+0x20 = 2 per dt */; launchFrames?: number /* port approximation for the +0x1cee stream launch signal */; whiffFrames?: number }. Machine constants (analog of SHARED_X): REPOS handled per-config; STREAM_RATE −1.0; GRAVITY 1.0; DECAY 0.9; SPEED_EPSILON 0.5; HITSTOP_DT 0.25; WINDUP_TIMER 60.0; AIR_RECOVERY_TIMER 120.0; COOLDOWN 8.0. Host hooks beyond the existing StreamContext: (1) pitch channel — actor.steerYaw (+0x18da) steered toward lock target each frame (clamp ±0x4000, no-target decay ×0.9) with velocity projection hSpeed=speed×cos(pitch), yVel=−speed×sin(pitch); (2) face-button input test (mash/hold, mask 0xf0f00); (3) hover bit toggle (controlWord |= 0x40 on launch, &= ~0x40 on ground exit) + upper-cue byte 0x2d; (4) group-0 slot-13 fall stream (args 0x14,1) for the P4 airborne branch; (5) hitstop (dt=0.25 tick substitution) when contact byte +0x1cef reads negative; (6) trail/afterimage FX hooks (renderer-side no-ops OK). Registration: five families of configs — fam1 (pl0700/pl0709), fam2A (pl0701/pl0708), fam2B (pl070c/pl070d — the only intra-family borg switch, keyed on borgNumber), fam3 (pl0702/pl070a), fam4 (pl0705/pl070b); all wired as actionTable[2] with all 5 variants → same machine.

findings.portImplications: This is a genuinely different shared machine from zz_00ff2bc_ (3-phase melee blink) — it is a 6-phase pitch-steered FLYING LANCE CHARGE with button-fueled flight extension, so it needs its own shared module (shared-flight-x.ts), not a SharedXConfig reuse. Key deltas vs shared-x-special.ts: (1) config is 11 data fields, zero callbacks — per-family porting is literally a config object away, including the one borg-switched family (fam2: pl0701/0708 vs pl070c/070d get different reposScale 0.95/0.92 and extendBudget 30/20); (2) new host capability needed: a pitch channel on the actor (reuse actor.steerYaw = +0x18da) steered toward the lock target with ±0x4000 clamp and 0.9 no-target decay, projected as hSpeed=speed*cos(pitch), yVel=-speed*sin(pitch) with gravityCoeff forced 0 during flight and restored from the descriptor at exits; (3) new input hook: face-button activity test (mask 0xf0f00 on the pad word) driving the 8→2-decaying transfer from extendBudget into flightTime — expose as ctx.isExtendInputActive() so the bridge can wire real pad state; (4) hitstop: P4 ticks the stream with dt=0.25 when the contact byte is negative — the stream VM tick needs an optional dt override; (5) exits use cooldown 8.0 (not 4.0) and the P4-airborne branch starts the generic group-0 slot-13 fall stream (args 0x14,1) — same one the bridge already models for air-falls; (6) two port approximations required until stream banks are byte-loaded, both precedented: launchFrames (stands in for the +0x1cee windup-anim launch signal) and the flight-time drain gate +0x1b01 (safe to approximate as always-on during phase 3, making base flight = flightTime/10 frames + extension). Registration shape: all four families wire actionTable[2] → createSharedFlightX(cfg) with all 5 variants identical; 10 borgs (pl0700-070d knight line) land in one commit once the module exists.
findings.summary: Transcription-grade dig of shared X-machine engine zz_0149708_ @0x80149708 (knight line, 4 families / 10 borgs, x-action 2) — a 6-phase pitch-steered flying lance charge, fully DERIVED. Phase table PTR_FUN_8033ed3c dumped (6 entries + NULL, bounded by the adjacent zz_0149ec8_ machine's table @0x8033ed58). All six phases transcribed with every float operand read from boot.dol: windup 60f (FLOAT_8043a384) with 0.95/0.92 blink-reposition, launch on stream signal +0x1cee loading an 11-field config (flight time ×10, button-extend budget ×10, transfer rate 8→floor 2, speed 0→30 @ accel 2/dt), flight phase projecting speed through steered pitch +0x18da (hSpeed=v·cos, yVel=−v·sin, homing via zz_006e1d0_ clamp ±0x4000), decel/impact phase with 0.9 decay, 0.5 speed epsilon, 0.25-dt hitstop on negative +0x1cef, and three exits (ground idle zz_006a474_, air-fall zz_006a5a4_ after 120f, landing zz_006a750_ cue 7) all seeding cooldown +0x694 = 8.0+dt. Config mechanism proven by raw disasm of all four wrappers (Ghidra dropped every r4 load): lis/addi → bl 0x80149708, configs 0x80308078 / 0x8032c814+0x8032c838 (family 2 is borg-switched on actor+0x3e8) / 0x8033c3d4 / 0x80373848, all five blocks dumped byte-exact and decoded — pure data, no callbacks. Port shape (SharedFlightXConfig) specified mirroring shared-x-special.ts. One drive-by correction: gred.ts:177's zz_00677b0_ return-sense comment is inverted.

claims.statement: Config passing: each family's action-3 entry is a two-stage wrapper. Stage 1 halves two family-local charge-meter shorts then bl stage 2; stage 2 loads r4 = config block via lis/addi and bl the engine. Family 1: FUN_8012750c @0x8012750c does lha/srawi/sth on +0x18e0 and +0x18e2 (each >>= 1), bl 0x80127544; zz_0127544_ @0x80127544 does lis r4,-0x7fcd (=0x80330000); addi r4,r4,0x3c8 -> r4=0x803303c8; bl 0x80177a3c. Family 2: FUN_8015bc50 @0x8015bc50 halves +0x1922 and +0x1926, bl 0x8015bc88; zz_015bc88_ @0x8015bc88 does lis r4,-0x7fcc (=0x80340000); addi r4,r4,0x5c34 -> r4=0x80345c34; bl 0x80177a3c. Ghidra dropped the r4 argument in both decompiles (zz_0127544_/zz_015bc88_ shown as plain calls). CONFIG LAYOUT (proven by the engine's loads off r31=r4): +0x00 u32 = group-2 stream slot (lwz r6,0(r31) @0x80177b48 -> zz_004beb8_ slot arg); +0x04 f32 = reposition/decay scale (lfs f1,4(r31) @0x80177b24/0x80177bbc/0x80177c8c — used in P0 blink AND P1/P2 per-tick decay); +0x08 ptr = on-hit callback (lwz r12,8(r31) @0x80177cc8, bctrl with r3=actor, NULL-checked). No other cfg offsets are read by the engine.

claims.statement: Port shape mirroring shared-x-special.ts: module `shared-charge3.ts` (suggested) with consts SHARED_CHARGE3 = { WINDUP_TIME: 30.0 /*FLOAT_8043ae00 -> +0x560*/, STREAM_RATE: -1.0 /*FLOAT_8043ae04*/, HIT_FORWARD_SPEED: 10.0 /*FLOAT_8043ae14 -> +0x44*/, HIT_DECEL: -0.3 /*FLOAT_8043ae18 -> +0x4c*/, RECOVERY_GRAVITY: 1.0 /*FLOAT_8043ae1c*/ } and interface SharedCharge3Config { slot: number /*cfg u32[0], group-2 stream slot; 1 for both families*/; repositionScale: number /*cfg f32[+4] = 0.98; P0 blink scale AND P1/P2 per-tick motion decay*/; onHit: ((actor, ctx) => void) | null /*cfg ptr[+8]; fires once on the P2 +0x1cef contact transition, BEFORE the 10/-0.3 speed writes*/; }. 4 phases: P0 setup (phase++ FIRST, timer=30, face, zero motion+turn rates, blink motion=(pos-target)*scale then pos+=motion, ground snap, startStream(actor, 0xf, group 2, cfg.slot, rate -1.0), +0x80c=0); P1 windup (tick gated on part-anim-hold analog, motion*=scale glide, timer-=dt, advance on timer<=0 OR facing-complete — the ROM has NO whiff fallback need here since the timer IS the fallback); P2 ride (tick, face, motion*=scale, on contactP0>0: phase++, cfg.onHit?.(), hSpeed=10, hDecel=-0.3); P3 recovery (tick, afterimage on +0x1d10 analog, on +0x1d0f stream event reload gravityCoeff from family anim-bank value (runtime data — needs a TUNED fallback constant), integratePhysics(1.0, actor, heading+180deg) flying backward, on landing-contact clear control bits &~3 and exit grounded?idleReset:airFall). Host hooks needed beyond shared-x-special's set: reversed-heading integration, the +0x1b03 tick-hold gate, and a per-family animBankGravity fallback. Family wiring: wrap as actionTable[3]; on entry (the wrapper layer, NOT the engine) halve the two family charge meters — expose as an optional preDispatch hook { halveChargeMeters: true } since the offsets are family-local (+0x18e0/+0x18e2 vs +0x1922/+0x1926).

claims.statement: This machine is DISTINCT from the shared X-tap machine zz_00ff2bc_ @0x800ff2bc already ported: different action slot (3 vs 2), different stream group (2 vs 4), no +0x6ea combo-cursor writes, config-driven reposition scale (0.98) instead of the pooled 0.95 constant, an explicit 30.0-unit windup timer phase absent from the X-tap, 4 phases vs 3, backward-flying recovery (heading+180) vs forward, and charge-meter halving at dispatch. Both share the +0x541/+0xcc prologue, the (pos-target)*k blink-away, the +0x1cef hit-callback pattern, and the zz_006a474_/zz_006a5a4_ exits.

Which pad input triggers action 3 for these families (X-charge release vs B-charge release): needs the +0x4ec command records (0x80330878 for pl0619, 0x803461e0/0x80345c40-region for family 2) and each borg descriptor's +0xb8 mode bytes decoded, per the ninja-dig initiator convention (zz_0069dd0_, chunk_0009.c:462).

The dead callback arms (borg 0x605 in FUN_8012756c; 0x611/0x622 in FUN_8015bcb0): are those borgs cut variants, or do their own families reach these spawn pairs via different code paths? (Their family binders were not located in this dig.)

Family-1 block +0x40..+0x6c holds four (+-50,0,+-50) XYZ float triplets and +0xa8..+0xb4 holds {0x0010,0x0064}, borg ids 0x604 and 0.98 floats — consumed by the OTHER action drivers (0x801271b0/0x8012745c/0x801274ac), not this engine; undecoded.
findings.portImplications: Port as a second parameterized shared machine alongside shared-x-special.ts (suggested families/shared-charge3.ts), wired as actionTable[3] for the pl0619 family and the pl061f/pl0625 family. Reuse existing rom/ primitives directly: startStream/tickStream (group 2, mask 0xf, rate -1.0), integratePhysics (with yaw = heading + 0x8000 — new: backward flight), vecSubtract/vecScale/vecAdd for the 0.98 blink+glide, dispatch exits already modeled by gred/ninja ports. Engine-level constants (30.0 windup, 10.0/-0.3 hit push, 1.0 recovery gravity) are DOL-derived and go in a module const; per-family config is only {slot:1, scale:0.98, onHit}. The onHit callbacks map cleanly onto the existing projectile pipeline: family 1 spawns 2x table-0x8035c1e0 kind-9 children (variant 1 for pl0619, ammo-gated via the already-ported zz_006dbe0_ cost gate = weapon-cell decrement); family 2 spawns 2x table-0x802d9758 kind-8 children (variants 17/18 for pl061f ammo-gated, 21/22 for pl0625 ungated) with initial velocity = owner speed x the 3-float row at 0x802d9794+variant*0x50 (dumpable from DOL for exact spawn vectors). Two approximations need labeling: the phase-1 tick gate (+0x1b03) can be approximated as always-tick (timer still bounds the phase), and the phase-3 anim-bank gravity reload needs a TUNED fallback until a Dolphin read pins blob+0x1b0+0x6c per borg. The charge-meter halving (+0x18e0/+0x18e2, +0x1922/+0x1926) belongs in the family wrapper layer, not the shared engine, mirroring the ROM split.
findings.summary: Transcription-grade dig of shared charge-release engine zz_0177a3c_ @0x80177a3c (x-action THREE for the pl0619 family and the pl061f/pl0625 family) is complete with all five deliverables DERIVED. The engine dispatches a 4-entry phase table PTR_FUN_80352af8 = [0x80177a8c, 0x80177b88, 0x80177c48, 0x80177d08] with r4=config flowing untouched from each family wrapper (exhaustive bl-scan: exactly two callers). Machine shape: P0 blink-away setup (motion=(pos-target)x0.98 cfg scale, pos+=motion, 30.0-unit timer, group-2 stream slot 1 at rate -1.0), P1 timed windup glide (tick gated on +0x1b03, timer-=dt, advance on expiry or facing-complete), P2 attack ride (on +0x1cef contact: phase++, per-family callback, +0x44=10.0/+0x4c=-0.3 push), P3 backward-flying recovery (gravity 1.0, yaw=heading+180deg, +0x1d0f anim-bank gravity reload, +0x1d10 afterimage, +0x1cee exit to zz_006a474_/zz_006a5a4_; no +0x558/+0x694/zz_006a750_ anywhere). Config layout {u32 group-2 slot, f32 repositionScale, ptr onHit} proven from the engine's r31-relative loads; both families' configs are identical (slot 1, 0.98) except the callback: FUN_8012756c spawns paired kind-9 table-0x8035c1e0 children (pl0619 variant 1, ammo-gated) via zz_0182fcc_, FUN_8015bcb0 spawns paired kind-8 table-0x802d9758 children (pl061f 17/18 gated, pl0625 21/22 ungated) via zz_0084600_. Both wrappers halve family-local charge meters on entry — the charge-release signature — and are bound at index 3 of their family root action tables (dispatchers FUN_80127174/FUN_8015b7a0 indexing +0x580).

claims.statement: Engine entry zz_0179fcc_ @0x80179fcc is a bare phase dispatcher with NO +0x541-style prologue: it loads r5 = 0x80352b64 (phase table), indexes by sign-extended byte actor+0x540 (<<2), and bctrl's the phase function. Critically, r4 (the per-family config pointer set by each wrapper) is passed through UNTOUCHED to the phase functions. Phase table PTR_FUN_80352b64 @0x80352b64 has exactly 3 live entries: [0]=0x8017a008, [1]=0x8017a130, [2]=0x8017a208; the next word @0x80352b70 is 0x0004000e (non-pointer data — a following config-style blob 0x0004000e / -600.0f / 80.0f / 0 / 1.0f), so phase indices must stay in 0..2.

(2) if lock-target ptr +0xcc == 0: +0x5ac = +0x72 (active yaw = heading) — NO built-in face call; facing is delegated to config callbacks. Config block LAYOUT (0x18 bytes), proven by the engine's loads off the config register: +0x00 u16 groundSlot (consumed P0 `(char)*param_2` → +0x6ea); +0x02 u16 airSlot (P0 `(char)param_2[1]` when +0x5e0&0x40); +0x04 f32 repositionScale (P0/P1/P2 `*(float *)(param_2+4)` → motion ×= scale each tick); +0x08 f32 (=0.1 in all three blocks; NO engine read — consumer unresolved); +0x0c ptr onSetup (P0 only, called after zero-init, before reposition); +0x10 ptr onSteer (called in P0 once AND P1 every tick); +0x14 ptr onHit (P1 contact transition only). Block size 0x18 confirmed for family 1 by the next structure starting at cfg+0x18 = 0x8030cf24 = PTR_FUN_8030cf24, family 1's action-4 variant table (FUN_800cfc38 dispatches +0x581 into it), and for family 2 by ctor-vtable slot +0x34 pointing at cfg2+0x18 = 0x8032f4b0.

findings.portImplications: This is the second shared X engine (zz_0179fcc_ @0x80179fcc, 3 phases @0x8017a008/0x8017a130/0x8017a208) — same architecture as zz_00ff2bc_ but a richer config (0x18 bytes: groundSlot u16, airSlot u16, repositionScale f32=0.95, reserved f32=0.1, onSetup ptr, onSteer ptr, onHit ptr) and different dynamics: gravity 1.0 integrates in BOTH phase 1 and 2, the 0.95 reposition slide persists through all three phases until onHit zeroes motion, cooldown is 1.0+dt (not 4.0), facing/steering is entirely callback-driven, and there is an aim-pitch channel (+0x18da) halved by the wrapper on entry and decayed ×0.9 per phase-2 tick. Port it as a NEW shared module (suggested families/shared-x-blink-strike.ts) parameterized by the interface in claim PORT-SHAPE; register fam1 (pl0601/09/17/1d, x-action 2, all 5 variants identical), fam2 (pl0604/18, x-action 3), fam3 (pl0613/27, x-action 3, aerial: onSetup sets yVel=4 and the 0x40 bit so it always uses air slot 2 and exits via upper-cue 7). Family onHits share the exact backdash numbers (hSpeed 40, yVel 20, decel −1, gravity from descriptor+0x6c) for fams 1/2; fam3 spawns without any backdash. Surface each family's spawners through the existing StreamContext.onFamilyProjectile hook (spawner addresses/types in claim SPAWNERS); child ballistics remain host residuals exactly like the ninja shuriken."
findings.summary: Transcription-grade dig of shared X-machine engine zz_0179fcc_ @0x80179fcc complete. Engine = bare 3-phase dispatcher (table PTR_FUN_80352b64 = [0x8017a008, 0x8017a130, 0x8017a208], indexed by +0x540, r4 config passed through untouched; no +0x541 prologue). Phases fully transcribed with all DOL-read constants: P0 = zero speeds, onSetup/onSteer callbacks, blink-reposition motion=(pos−target)×cfg.scale(0.95), ground snap, cfg slot select (air fork on +0x5e0&0x40, NO slot increment), stream start (rate −1.0, mask 0xf, group 4); P1 = tick + per-tick onSteer + reposition tail + gravity-1.0 integration + contact +0x1cef>0 → phase 2 + onHit + afterimage gate 3.0; P2 = pitch +0x18da ×0.9 decay, +0x1d0f event reloads +0x50 from descriptor+0x6c, landing damp zz_006ed8c_(0.1), +0x1cee-gated three-way exit (zz_006a5a4_/zz_006a474_/zz_006a750_(7)), cooldown +0x694 = 1.0+dt. Config mechanism proven by raw disasm of all three wrappers (lis/addi r4 → cfg → bl engine; Ghidra drops r4): fam1 wrapper 0x800cf9cc→cfg 0x8030cf0c {0,1,0.95,·,NULL,0x800cfa00,0x800cfa38} behind a 5-entry all-identical variant table @0x8030cef8; fam2 0x80126110→0x8032f498 {1,2,0.95,·,NULL,0x80126150,0x801261a0}; fam3 0x8018cb68→0x8036551c {1,2,0.95,·,0x8018cba8,0x8018cbc0,0x8018cc10}. All 7 family callbacks transcribed (face-away backdash 40/20/−1 for fams 1-2, fam3 aerial rise 4.0 + 0x40 bit, borg switches matching the given member lists, ammo gates zz_006dbe0_(2,1,1), four child spawners with type codes 1/2/0x5c/0x5a and update-fn pointers). Ctors (missing from Ghidra; raw-disassembled) prove x-action 2/3/3 wiring via 16-word vtable copies whose slot 0 root dispatchers index PTR_FUN_8030ce34/8032f41c/8036546c by +0x580. Port shape delivered as SharedXBlinkStrikeConfig mirroring shared-x-special.ts.

claims.statement: Engine entry zz_017a374_ @0x8017a374 is a bare 2-entry phase dispatcher with NO +0x541 prologue (unlike zz_00ff2bc_): raw disasm 0x8017a37c `addi r5, r13, -0x6df0` resolves the phase table to 0x8043b5a0-0x6df0 = 0x804347b0; 0x8017a384-98 `lbz r0,0x540(r3); extsb; slwi 2; lwzx r12,r5,r0; mtctr; bctrl`. r3=actor and r4=config are PRESERVED into the phase call, so every phase function receives (actor, cfg) — Ghidra's decomp `(*(code*)(&PTR_FUN_804347b0)[*(char*)(param_1+0x540)])()` dropped both args.

claims.statement: Phase table @0x804347b0 has exactly 2 entries: [0]=0x8017a3ac (phase 0, windup+stream start), [1]=0x8017a4a8 (phase 1, tick+morph+exit). The next word 0x804347b8 = 0x002e0000 is not a code pointer, bounding the table at 2.

Stream rate operand: lfs f1,-0x3c1c(r2) with r2=0x8043ea20 -> FLOAT_8043ae04 = 0xbf800000 = -1.0.

claims.statement: SharedXConfig-analog for this machine (mirror of shared-x-special.ts): interface SharedMorphXConfig { groundSlot: number /* cfg s8@0, group-4 stream slot when grounded */; airSlot: number /* cfg s8@1 */; groundForm: number /* cfg s16@2, borg id morphed into when grounded */; airForm: number /* cfg s16@4 */; flightExitForm: number /* cfg s16@6; -1 = never; morph target === this -> exit dispatchUpperBodyCue(actor, 0x1b) instead of idle/air-fall */; repositionScale: number /* cfg f32@8; 0.98 for all nine ROM blocks */; preEngine?: (actor) => void /* family-4 wrapper work: borg 0x611 halves +0x1922/+0x1926; 0x622/0x625 halve +0x18e0/+0x18e2 */ }. Host hooks needed beyond the existing StreamContext: (1) morphActor(actor, borgId, playerSlot) — the zz_006a8c0_ analog: set borg number, bump form counter (+0x3f0), carry skin bytes (+0x3e4/+0x3e7), swap model/descriptor, then run the FUN_800562b8-analog weapon/ammo re-init and the zz_017a608_-analog gun-state wipe; (2) playSfx(0xb2) at phase 0; (3) clearChainCancelMasks(0xff); (4) the three existing exits. Machine skeleton: phase 0 = blink-away (motion=(pos-target)*0.98, pos+=motion), zero-nothing (unlike zz_00ff2bc_ it does NOT zero +0x44..+0x50), pick slot/form by grounded, startStream(0xf, group 4, slot, rate -1.0), remember pendingForm (+0x548); phase 1 = tickStream(0xf), motion*=0.98 continue, on stream END (the +0x1cee event — the same whiff-frames approximation gred.ts/shared-x-special.ts use applies, since stream banks are not byte-loaded) do morph + wipe + controlWord&=~3 + exit select. No cooldown seed, no timers in the shared engine. pl061a (PROTO EAGLE) must instead be registered with a bespoke 4-phase machine (see CFG-FAM-EAGLE-061A-BESPOKE) whose hooks are the existing ammo gate (slot 2, cost 1), projectile spawns types 0xc/0xd via the onFamilyProjectile hook, gravity integratePhysics(1.0), and cooldowns 16.0 (no-ammo) / 8.0 (landing) + dt.

findings.portImplications: This engine is NOT another SharedXConfig clone — it is the Machine-Borg TRANSFORM X-special. Port it as a second shared machine (e.g. families/shared-morph-x.ts) with SharedMorphXConfig {groundSlot, airSlot, groundForm, airForm, flightExitForm, repositionScale, preEngine?}. Machine shape: 2 phases only. P0: blink-away reposition (motion=(pos−lockTarget)×0.98, pos+=motion — NOTE scale comes from config, and unlike zz_00ff2bc_ it does NOT zero +0x44..+0x50), SFX 0xb2, clear chain-cancel masks, pick {slot, pendingForm} by grounded, startStream(mask 0xf, group 4, slot, rate −1.0). P1: tickStream(0xf), reposition continue ×0.98, per-tick gun-state decay (+0x18da>>=1, +0x1d64/+0x1d68 ×0.9 truncated), and on stream END (+0x1cee — use the same whiff-frames stand-in as shared-x-special.ts since stream banks aren't byte-loaded) do: morphActor(pendingForm) [swap borg id, bump form counter, carry skin bytes, weapon/ammo re-init per FUN_800562b8, zero the 15 gun-state fields per zz_017a608_], controlWord&=~3, then exit = (pendingForm===flightExitForm) ? dispatchUpperBodyCue(0x1b) : grounded ? groundIdleReset : airFall. No cooldown/timers in the shared engine. Registrations: TITAN pl0604/pl0618→{0,0,0x605,0x605,−1}; EAGLE pl0606→{0,0,0x607,0x607,0x607}; VICTORY KING 0x610→{0,1,0x611,0x612,0x612}; 0x611→pre-halve(+0x1922/+0x1926)+{0,1,0x610,0x612,0x612}; DUKE 0x621↔0x622 {0,0,…,−1}; BARON 0x623→{1,1,0x624,0x624,0x624}; VICTORY MACHINE 0x625→pre-halve(+0x18e0/+0x18e2)+{1,1,0x626,0x626,0x626}; PANTHER pl0613/pl0627→{0,0,0x614,0x614,−1}. PROTO EAGLE pl061a needs a separate bespoke 4-phase gun machine (constants: reposition 0.95, gravity 1.0, cooldown 16.0 no-ammo / 8.0 landing + dt, heat decay 0.9, ammo slot 2 cost 1, projectile types 0xc/0xd via onFamilyProjectile). Big blocker to flag: the morph targets are borg ids with no borgs.json entries (vehicle forms) — the host must be able to instantiate/re-skin those alt-form actors (and their families own the morph-back X) before this port is user-visible; until then the honest option is registering the machine with a no-op morph hook.
findings.summary: Engine 0x8017a374 decoded transcription-grade: it is the Machine-Borg line's shared TRANSFORM (morph) X-special, a 2-phase machine (table 0x804347b0 = [0x8017a3ac, 0x8017a4a8]) whose r4 config (12 bytes, stride 0xc: s8 groundSlot, s8 airSlot, s16 groundForm, s16 airForm, s16 flightExitForm, f32 repositionScale=0.98) selects a group-4 stream slot and a target borg id; phase 1 decays gun-state fields, and on stream end (+0x1cee) swaps the actor's borg number via zz_006a8c0_ (Ghidra dropped its 3rd arg r5=+0x3ec), wipes 15 gun-state fields + re-inits weapon/ammo (zz_017a608_/FUN_800562b8), and exits via upper cue 0x1b (when morphing to the flight form), ground idle, or air-fall. All nine per-borg config blocks dumped and decoded (TITAN 0x604/0x618→0x605; EAGLE 0x606→0x607; VICTORY KING 0x610↔0x611 with shared air form 0x612; DUKE 0x621↔0x622; BARON 0x623→0x624; VICTORY MACHINE 0x625→0x626; PANTHER 0x613/0x627→0x614), wrapper→engine bl proven by raw disasm for every family, family-4 wrappers pre-halve heat fields, and PROTO EAGLE pl061a was found to bypass the engine entirely with a bespoke 4-phase gun machine (phase table 0x803319ec, projectiles 0xc/0xd, cooldowns 16/8) — fully transcribed. PROTO KING/VICTORY TANK have dead X branches (UNRESOLVED why).

claims.statement: Engine 0x80188da4 (FUN_80188da4) is a bare phase dispatcher with NO +0x541-style prologue: it loads phase byte actor+0x540, sign-extends, and bctrl's through the pointer table at 0x80362674. Unlike zz_00ff2bc_ there is no 'if(+0x541){+0xcc=0}' target-invalidation prologue and no r4 config register — the phase functions receive only the actor in r3.

claims.statement: The +0x4e8 data block (0x80362690 / 0x8037a9f0, 16 bytes '00 01 01 00 00 0a 00 00 00 10 01 00 00 0a ff 00', identical content in both families) is installed where other families put command-mode/aux data, but its field semantics were not traced to a consumer in this dig; likewise the +0x27c per-family block (0x803628f0/0x8037aad8, first 0x40 bytes identical '00 0a 00 0a 0a 32 00 00 01 01 80 02 00 05 00 00 ...') was not layout-decoded — reading past +0x40 of the pl0d01 copy runs into an unrelated 6-pointer table (0x80189320..0x801896b4, likely the pl0d03 sibling family's action code). Neither block is read by the x-action-1 engine or its phases, so neither blocks the port.

findings.portImplications: New shared machine, NOT a SharedXConfig reuse: unlike zz_00ff2bc_ (config-block-in-r4 pattern), the pl0d pair binds one engine (0x80188da4) through one shared action table (0x80362658) installed by both ctors, and forks per-family INSIDE phase 1 on borg id (0x0d01→satellite variant 0, else 1). Port as packages/combat/src/families/satellite-x-special.ts exporting createSatelliteXSpecial(cfg) with cfg = { satelliteVariant: 0|1, onDeploySatellite(actor, variant) } and constants { windup 0.0, recovery 30.0, ammo slot 2 cost 1, exit cue 0x1b, no cooldown seed } — wire as actionTable[1] for BOTH pl0d01 and pl0d05; command types 2 AND 5 both route to it (cue 0x2d → full-body 61). Registration needs the shared cue table 0x803627b8 (family-0-like rows, sparse: 0,9-11,27,28,35,36,43-47). The X payload is a spawned homing satellite child (type 0x4d): both variants share one record — muzzle bone 1 offset (0,-80,-10), launch speed 10 with dir.y pre-tilt -0.1, then vy=-5.0 with accel -0.1/frame (this IS the 'gravity -0.1' satellite drift — it lives in the CHILD record, not actor physics; the actor never moves during the special), homing re-steer every 4 frames at rate 3.0 toward lockTarget pos, life 600 frames, 10-shot budget, despawn on owner death/borg-swap. Host hooks needed: (1) StreamContext.onFamilyProjectile-style satellite spawn hook, (2) a family-update hook clearing the deployed latch (+0x145) when ammo slot 2 refills (max @+0x78e+slot*8, cur @+0x774+slot*8) — re-deploy gating is ammo-regen-driven, not timer-driven, (3) button-held bit (+0x5bc & 0x200) only needed if the B actions (turret burst action 0 with 0.2-rate pitch-only aim clamp [0,0x4000] and 10-round hold-fire; alternating pod launcher action 2 recs 0x10/0x11 muzzles (-15.48,0,41.44)/(34.43,0,57.15) speed 15) are ported in the same pass. No whiffFrames approximation is required anywhere — the machine is purely timer-driven (30.0 recovery), the first ported family machine with zero stream-VM dependency.
findings.summary: Transcription-grade dig complete for engine 0x80188da4 (pl0d01/pl0d05 satellite pair, x-action 1). Architecture finding: 0x80362674 is the shared PHASE TABLE [0x80188de0, 0x80188df8, 0x80188e7c] and there are NO per-family wrappers at all — both Ghidra-missed ctors (0x8018882c/0x8018890c, raw-disassembled) copy 16-slot blocks (0x803625d8/0x80362618) whose +0x4b4 is the same root dispatcher FUN_80188b24 → shared action table 0x80362658 with entry[1] = the engine; per-family variation is a runtime borg-id switch (s16 +0x3e8 == 0x0d01) inside phase 1 selecting satellite variant 0/1. The X machine is purely timer-driven (windup 0.0, recovery 30.0 = FLOAT_8043afb0, dt at +0x1dc8): no streams, no contact flags, no actor velocity/gravity writes, exit via zz_006a53c_(actor,0) → full-body cue 0x1b → idle state 0 with NO +0x694 cooldown. Payload: ammo-slot-2-gated (zz_006dbe0_(actor,2,1,1), latch +0x145 cleared when slot-2 ammo refills in family update FUN_80188a64) spawn of homing-satellite child type 0x4d via zz_00fb220_ — child records dumped (life 600, fire interval 4, speed 10, vy -5, y-accel -0.1 = the satellite drift, homing rate 3.0, muzzle bone 1 @(0,-80,-10); variants byte-identical except one setup s16). Command tables both route types 2 AND 5 → cue 0x2d/action 1; B commands diverge (pl0d01→action 2 pod launcher type 0x4c; pl0d05→action 0 pitch-clamped turret burst via zz_0082824_ recs 0x10/0x11, dumped). Sibling actions 0/2 fully transcribed, cue table 0x803627b8 dumped, wrapper-proof chains established for both families. 20 claims: 17 DERIVED, 2 LIKELY, 1 UNRESOLVED.

claims.statement: PROJECTILE (deliverable 3c): zz_007db5c_(actor, 5) selects record 5 of the family shot table 0x802d6d68 (stride 0x38) @0x802d6e80. Raw bytes: `00020b01 425c0000 00000000 00000000 42200000 00000004 43fa0000 00000000 40f00000 3e19999a 3e99999a 0000000c 40000000 ffffffff`. Decode (fields the spawner provably reads, plus stride-layout knowns): u16+0x00 texId=2; byte+0x02 muzzle bone=0x0b (11) [spawner: `(char)(&DAT_802d6d6a)[iVar3] * 0x30 + 0x8d4` matrix pick]; byte+0x03 kind=1; f32 muzzle offset (55.0, 0.0, 0.0) @+0x04/08/0c [spawner: PSMTXMultVec with `(float *)(iVar3 + -0x7fd29294)` = 0x802d6d6c+iVar3]; f32+0x10=40.0; u32+0x14=4; f32+0x18=500.0; f32+0x1c=0.0; f32+0x20=7.5; f32+0x24=0.15; f32+0x28=0.3; byte+0x2c=0 → NO owner+0x7b0 position add [spawner: `if ((&DAT_802d6d94)[iVar3] != '\0') PSVECAdd(...+0x7b0...)`]; s16+0x2e=0x000c = borg-number gate [spawner: `if (*(short *)(param_1 + 1000) == *(short *)(&DAT_802d6d96 + iVar3))` — a REDUNDANT second gate: even if called for pl0008 the spawn no-ops]; f32+0x30=2.0; u32+0x34=0xffffffff (no bank flags). claims.statement: BORG SWITCHES pl0008 vs pl000c (deliverable 4): (1) Ctor descriptor swap: +0x4b0 = 0x803765e0 (pl0008) vs 0x803766f8 (pl000c), keyed on s16+0x3e8 == 0xc. (2) THE BIG ONE — descriptor +0xb8 command mode bytes: pl0008 @0x80376698 = `01 00 ff ff ff ff` → command type 2 (X-special) is DISABLED for pl0008 (0xff rejected by initiator zz_0069dd0_, chunk_0009.c:462 per the verified ninja decode); pl000c @0x803767b0 = `01 00 01 ff ff ff` → X live with 2-D (situation×direction) records; charge types 3/4/5 disabled for both. So the shared-X machine is command-reachable ONLY for pl000c; for pl0008 the entire action-2 path is dead code (same pattern as ninja's dead actions 3/4). (3) onHit callback spawns type-5 shot only when borg == 0xc. (4) Spawner record-5 gate s16+0x2e == 0xc (redundant belt-and-suspenders). Full descriptor diff (0x118 bytes, assuming the two blocks are same-shape): 10 bytes differ — +0xba (ff→01, the X enable), +0xca (20→16), +0xcc (00→0a), +0xd0 (20→1c), +0xd2 (00→04), +0xd6 (20→0c), +0xd8 (00→14), +0xf8/f9/fa (00 00 00 → 45 1c 40); semantics of the non-0xba bytes UNRESOLVED (plausibly ammo/tuning — pl0008's zeros at +0xf8-0xfa fit 'no X ammo', but that is a guess).

claims.statement: PORT SHAPE (deliverable 4/interface): reuses SharedXConfig from packages/combat/src/families/shared-x-special.ts verbatim — no new interface fields needed. Family config instance: { groundSlot: 0, airSlot: 1, onHit: deathBorgOnHit, whiffFrames: TUNED }. deathBorgOnHit(actor, ctx): (a) pop — actor.lockYaw(+0x5ae) = heading(+0x72) − 0x8000; hSpeed(+0x44)=10.0; yVel(+0x48)=15.0; hDecel(+0x4c)=0.0; gravityCoeff(+0x50)=−1.0 (ROM gates the pop on stream-event +0x1d0f; ninja.ts precedent applies for how the port models that gate); (b) ammo gate slot 2 cost 1 (host hook, same as ninja.ts); on success, ONLY for borgId 'pl000c', surface the projectile via the existing StreamContext.onFamilyProjectile hook with (table 0x802d6d68, variant 5) — bone 11, muzzle (55,0,0), texId 2, kind 1, no bank flags, no +0x7b0 offset. Registration mirroring families/ninja.ts: register both pl0008 and pl000c with actionTable[2] = createSharedXSpecial(cfg, ctx), but the host command layer must honor that pl0008's X command is descriptor-disabled (mode byte 0xff) — cleanest is to register the family X only for pl000c, or gate at the command initiator as the ROM does.

zz_006dbe0_ 4th argument (=1) semantics — same open as the ninja dig; also whether a successful gate call CONSUMES ammo for pl0008 despite no spawn (the callback calls it unconditionally before the borg check).

Non-mode-byte descriptor diffs (+0xca/+0xcc/+0xd0/+0xd2/+0xd6/+0xd8 and +0xf8..+0xfa: pl000c values 16/0a/1c/04/0c/14/45/1c/40) — field semantics unknown; +0xf8-0xfa zeros for pl0008 plausibly 'no X ammo' but unverified; also the 0x118 same-shape/stride assumption for the diff window.

Action table @0x8037643c upper bound — entries [3]=0x8019ee34, [4]=[5]=0x8019f0a8, [6]=[7]=0x8019f314 dumped but not verified as table members (types 3-5 are command-disabled for both borgs regardless).

Shot record float semantics beyond spawner-proven fields (+0x10=40.0, +0x14=4, +0x18=500.0, +0x20=7.5, +0x24=0.15, +0x28=0.3, +0x30=2.0) — consumed by child update FUN_8007dcec (still unread; carried from nn-family-decode §8).
What the two pointers 0x801a31d8 (×2) at 0x80435758 belong to (adjacent structure after the 8-byte config).
findings.portImplications: Zero new engine work: this family is a pure SharedXConfig instantiation of the already-ported shared-x-special.ts machine. Concretely: (1) new families/death-borg.ts (or extend a shared registry) with cfg { groundSlot: 0, airSlot: 1, onHit } — slots identical to ninja so stream wiring is already exercised; (2) onHit = ninja-backflip-shaped pop with the SAME four constants (10.0 / 15.0 / 0.0 / −1.0, yaw −0x8000) — can literally share ninja.ts's pop helper, only the projectile branch differs; (3) projectile: ammo slot 2 cost 1, then pl000c-ONLY spawn of table 0x802d6d68 variant 5 through the existing StreamContext.onFamilyProjectile hook (variant 5 is already present in shotVariantKinds.json as kind 1 / borg 0xc, so the host-side flight-visual decode needs no new data); pl0008 spawns nothing (still calls the ammo gate in ROM). (4) CRITICAL gating: pl0008's X-special is COMMAND-DISABLED at the descriptor (mode byte +0xb8[2] = 0xff vs pl000c's 0x01) — register the X action only for pl000c, or replicate the initiator's mode-byte rejection; wiring shared-X for pl0008 would add a move the real game never allows. whiffFrames stays a TUNED port-side value (default 60) as in gred.ts/ninja.ts. Cue-table/bridge work not covered here (ctor binds +0x27c = 0x80376960, LIKELY this family's 48x2 cue table — separate dump needed before bridging, same shape as family-0 @0x802d3ff8).
findings.summary: Confirmed end-to-end: Death Borg Alpha family (pl0008 DEATH BORG ALPHA / pl000c DEATH BORG ALPHA II, ctor zz_019e9a4_ @0x8019e9a4) routes X-action 2 through action table @0x8037643c[2] = 0x801a0414, whose inner wrapper 0x801a0434 loads r4 = r13−0x5e50 = 0x80435750 and bl's the already-ported shared X machine zz_00ff2bc_ @0x800ff2bc (raw-disasm proven; Ghidra dropped the r4 arg). Config @0x80435750 = {groundSlot 0, airSlot 1, onHit 0x801a0458} — same slots as ninja. The onHit callback zz_01a0458_ is a ninja-shaped backflip pop (yaw −0x8000; +0x44=10.0, +0x48=15.0, +0x4c=0.0, +0x50=−1.0 — byte-identical values from a different literal pool: FLOAT_8043b51c/8043b538/8043b4d0/8043b4cc) plus ammo gate zz_006dbe0_(actor,2,1,1) and, ONLY for borg 0xc (pl000c), shot spawn zz_007db5c_(actor,5) → table 0x802d6d68 record 5 @0x802d6e80 (bone 11, muzzle (55,0,0), texId 2, kind 1, borg-gate 0xc, no bank flags — identical ballistics to ninja's record 0). Key borg switch beyond the callback: pl0008's descriptor (0x803765e0) mode bytes `01 00 ff ff ff ff` DISABLE the X-special entirely; only pl000c (descriptor 0x803766f8, `01 00 01 ff ff ff`) can fire it. Port is a pure SharedXConfig instantiation — no engine changes.

verdicts.note: All evidence re-derived: 8019e9a4_movement-physics_candidate_8019e9a4.c:6 binds +0x4b4 = PTR_zz_019edbc__803763e8; dol read_u32(0x803763e8)=0x8019edbc; lines 23/29-31 bind +0x4b0=&DAT_803765e0 and swap to &DAT_803766f8 when *(short*)(param_1+1000)==0xc (1000=0x3e8, Ghidra decimal print confirmed); borgs.json:156-157 pl0008=DEATH BORG ALPHA, :232-233 pl000c=DEATH BORG ALPHA II, both tribe Death Borg; callback disasm 0x801a04c4 lha r0,0x3e8(r31); cmpwi r0,0xc verified. STRONGER PROOF FOUND (see extraFindings): the per-borg family-ctor pointer table @0x802d2e98 has [8]@0x802d2eb8 and [0xc]@0x802d2ec8 = 0x8019e9a4 — the ONLY two data refs to the ctor in the DOL — and its [0]/[0xa] = 0x8006f4f8 (ninja pl0000/pl000a per nn-family-decode-2026-07-06.md:3), directly binding this ctor to exactly pl0008+pl000c.

8019edbc_zz_019edbc.c:6 quote exact: `(*(code *)(&PTR_zz_019edf8__8037643c)[*(char *)(param_1 + 0x580)])();`. DOL dump @0x8037643c re-read: [0]=0x8019edf8 [1]=0x8019f8ec [2]=0x801a0414 [3]=0x8019ee34 [4]=0x8019f0a8 [5]=0x8019f0a8 [6]=0x8019f314 [7]=0x8019f314 — byte-identical to the claim. Bounds hedge for entries past [2] is fair (no bounding symbol in the .map at 0x8037643c; nearest-symbol lookup returns an unrelated far symbol), and the descriptor mode bytes (verified under borg-switches) do show only types 0/1/2 command-enabled.

Raw disasm re-derived. Driver 0x801a0414-0x801a0430 is exactly stwu/mflr/stw/bl 0x801a0434/lwz/mtlr/addi/blr — 8 instructions, no variant sub-dispatch, no read of +0x581. Wrapper 0x801a0434-0x801a0454 is exactly 9 instructions with 0x801a043c `388da1b0 addi r4, r13, -0x5e50` and 0x801a0444 `4bf5ee79 bl 0x800ff2bc <zz_00ff2bc_>`. r13=0x8043b5a0 re-derived from raw disasm 0x80003334-38 (lis r13,0x8043; ori 0xb5a0); 0x8043b5a0-0x5e50=0x80435750 arithmetic verified. Both organized/game/ui decompiles (801a0414_zz_01a0414.c:6, 801a0434_zz_01a0434.c:6) show the arg-less calls; _map.tsv confirms both from chunk_0049.c. Ninja parallel (r13-0x7d38=0x80433868) arithmetic verified.

800ff2bc_zz_00ff2bc.c:6-9 quote exact (prologue `if (+0x541) +0xcc=0;` then dispatch through PTR_zz_00ff30c__8031f000 by s8 +0x540). DOL @0x8031f000 re-read: 0x800ff30c, 0x800ff418, 0x800ff4d4, 0x00000000. packages/combat/src/families/shared-x-special.ts exists, 175 lines, SHARED_X constants 0.95/-1.0/1.0/4.0/3.0; all five FLOAT_804392d0/b8/d4/d8/c8 re-read from DOL this session = 0.95(f32)/-1.0/1.0/4.0/3.0. Wording nit only: the engine dispatch is `lwzx r12/mtctr/bctrl` (a call that returns, not a literal tail-jump); r4 is untouched from entry to bctrl (disasm 0x800ff2c8-0x800ff2f8), so the substance holds.

Wrapper disasm verified (see wrapper-proof). Engine passes r4 through untouched to the phase call (raw disasm 0x800ff2bc-0x800ff2f8: r4 never written before bctrl). P0 quotes exact at 800ff30c_force-setup_candidate_800ff30c.c:28-31 and :35; stream rate dVar3=(double)FLOAT_804392b8 and DOL read_f32(0x804392b8)=-1.0 confirmed; note the call uses the PRE-increment slot (cVar1) while +0x6ea stores slot+1 — claim wording 'slot++ and zz_004beb8_(...,slot,...)' is order-ambiguous but the cited port (shared-x-special.ts:98-101) models the ROM order correctly. P1 quote exact at 800ff418_knockback-targeting_candidate_800ff418.c:13-17 (onHit NULL-checked, called with actor only). P2 800ff4d4_force-setup_candidate_800ff4d4.c re-read: zero uses of param_2. Layout {u16 +0, u16 +2, ptr +4} = 8 bytes matches every proven read.

DOL bytes @0x80435750 re-read: 00000001 801a0458 801a31d8 801a31d8 — groundSlot=0, airSlot=1, onHit=0x801a0458, followed by two 0x801a31d8 words, exactly as claimed. Engine provably reads only +0x00/+0x02/+0x04 (see config-mechanism), so the 8-byte boundary is sound. Ninja config @0x80433868 re-dumped this session: 00000001 800715f8 — slots identical (0/1), only callback differs. 0x801a0458 verified as a real function: raw disasm shows stwu/mflr/stw/stw r31/mr r31,r3 prologue then `88031d0f lbz r0, 0x1d0f(r3)` — the stream-event gate, same shape as ninja's zz_00715f8_.

Fully re-derived from both lenses. Decomp 801a0458_force-setup_candidate_801a0458.c body matches the quote verbatim (minor citation nit: body is lines 15-26, claim said 15-27). Raw disasm re-derived 0x801a045c-0x801a04ec: +0x1d0f gate (lbz/extsb./beq 0x801a04a8); pop block 0x801a0478-0x801a04a4 exactly as quoted (lha r3,0x72(r31); addis r3,r3,1; addi r0,r3,-0x8000; sth r0,0x5ae(r31); stfs f3->0x44, f2->0x4c, f1->0x48, f0->0x50) — addis+addi nets +0x8000 mod 2^16, identical to -0x8000 in 16-bit BAM, agreeing with Ghidra and the claim. Float operands -0x3504/-0x3550/-0x34e8/-0x3554 vs r2=0x8043ea20 (r2 re-derived from raw disasm 0x8000332c-30) resolve to 0x8043b51c/0x8043b4d0/0x8043b538/0x8043b4cc; DOL reads = 10.0/0.0/15.0/-1.0 → +0x44=10, +0x48=15, +0x4c=0, +0x50=-1 exactly as claimed. Ammo gate OUTSIDE the +0x1d0f gate confirmed (beq target 0x801a04a8 is the ammo-call setup): li r4,2; li r5,1; li r6,1; bl 0x8006dbe0; cmpwi r3,0; beq; lha r0,0x3e8(r31); cmpwi r0,0xc; bne; li r4,5; bl 0x8007db5c. No other borg branches exist in the function. Ninja comparison verified at 800715f8_force-setup_candidate_800715f8.c:24-38 (branches 0->type0, 3->type1 +type2 if +0x7c8, 10->type3).

Record math verified: 0x802d6d68 + 5*0x38 = 0x802d6e80. DOL dump of rec5 byte-identical to the claimed hex; field decode matches every value (texId=2, bone=0x0b, kind=1, muzzle (55,0,0), 40.0, 4, 500.0, 0.0, 7.5, 0.15, 0.3, +0x2c=0, s16+0x2e=0x000c, 2.0, 0xffffffff). Spawner decomp 8007db5c_knockback-targeting_candidate_8007db5c.c re-read: :10 `iVar3 = (uint)param_2 * 0x38`; :11 borg gate vs DAT_802d6d96 (=base+0x2e); :12 zz_0088aa0_(param_1,3,8,0,1); :15-18 [0x10]=0xf, [0x11]=param_2, +0xc=FUN_8007dcec, +0x10c=FUN_8007e698; :36 bone byte DAT_802d6d6a*0x30+0x8d4; :37 muzzle ptr iVar3+-0x7fd29294 = 0x802d6d6c+iVar3 (two's-complement verified); :38-40 +0x2c flag gates PSVECAdd of owner+0x7b0. The gate makes record-5 spawn a no-op for any borg != 0xc, confirming the redundant-gate reading. rec0 vs rec5 diff re-computed: exactly 3 bytes — +0x01 (0e->02, texId 14 vs 2), +0x2f (00->0c, borg gate), +0x35 (01->ff). shotVariantKinds.json fireSiteResolutions[20] matches verbatim (table 0x802d6d68, variant 5, kind 1, fireFn zz_01a0458_, viaSpawner zz_007db5c_, borgIds ['0xc'], chunkLine chunk_0049.c:2330). One semantics caveat: 'no bank flags' for +0x34=0xffffffff is inherited from the prior flight-visual decode convention, not re-proven here; the raw value is DERIVED.

All bytes re-read from DOL: pl0008 mode bytes @0x803765e0+0xb8 (=0x80376698) = 01 00 ff ff ff ff; pl000c @0x803766f8+0xb8 (=0x803767b0) = 01 00 01 ff ff ff. The 0xff-rejection semantics re-confirmed DIRECTLY this session in chunk_0009.c (zz_0069dd0_ @0x80069dd0, ~line 462): `if (*(char *)(*(int *)(param_1+0x4b0) + (int)*(char *)(param_1+0x585) + 0xb8) == -1) uVar2 = 0;` — descriptor +0xb8 indexed by command type +0x585, -1 rejected; and the ==1 branch routes to the 2-D reader zz_0069ea4_, matching the 01=2D/00=1D semantics from nn-family-decode-2026-07-06.md §5. Full 0x118 diff re-computed: exactly 10 differing bytes at +0xba(ff->01), +0xca(20->16), +0xcc(00->0a), +0xd0(20->1c), +0xd2(00->04), +0xd6(20->0c), +0xd8(00->14), +0xf8/f9/fa(00,00,00 -> 45,1c,40); first 0x20 bytes byte-identical and equal to the quoted 0000030000000000000000000b14140c... prefix. The same-shape assumption is additionally supported by 0x803766f8-0x803765e0 = 0x118 exactly (the two descriptors are adjacent at that stride). Ctor swap (file lines 29-31), callback 0xc gate (disasm), and spawner record gate all re-verified. UNRESOLVED labels on the non-0xba diff bytes are appropriately hedged.

This is a port plan; all its factual anchors verify. SharedXConfig at shared-x-special.ts:35-48 has exactly {groundSlot, airSlot, onHit, whiffFrames?} — sufficient for {0, 1, deathBorgOnHit, TUNED} with no new fields. ninja.ts:31-72 provides the exact precedent claimed: NINJA_X constants block, unconditional-pop approximation of the +0x1d0f gate documented at lines 54-57, ammo via host hook, projectile surfaced through ctx.onFamilyProjectile (line 71), registration of two borgs through one family module (header lines 1-22). All numeric constants (slots 0/1, pop 10/15/0/-1, table 0x802d6d68 variant 5, bone 11, muzzle (55,0,0), texId 2, kind 1, +0x2c=0, +0x34=0xffffffff) trace to the DOL reads confirmed in the other claims. The pl0008 X-disabled guidance follows from the confirmed +0xba=0xff mode byte and the confirmed zz_0069dd0_ rejection path.

Ninja config block @0x80433868 re-dumped this session: bytes = 00 00 00 01 80 07 15 f8 (groundSlot 0, airSlot 1, onHit 0x800715f8) — confirms the config-dump claim's 'slots identical to ninja, only the callback differs' with fresh bytes rather than citing the prior doc.

Precision note on config-mechanism P0: zz_004beb8_ is called with the PRE-increment slot (cVar1 = the cfg slot value) and +0x6ea then holds slot+1 (800ff30c...c:32-35). The claim's phrasing 'slot++ and zz_004beb8_(..., slot, ...)' is order-ambiguous; the cited port shared-x-special.ts:98-101 (`streamSlot = slot + 1; startStream(..., slot, ...)`) models the ROM order correctly, so no port impact.

Precision note on machine/config claims: the engine's phase dispatch at 0x800ff2f0-0x800ff2f8 is `lwzx r12,r5,r0; mtctr r12; bctrl` — a call that returns, not a literal tail-dispatch. r4 is never written between function entry and the bctrl, so the claim's substance (config pointer arrives as phase param_2) is verified; only the 'tail-dispatch' wording is imprecise.
Citation nit: the onHit callback body in organized/game/battle/force-setup/801a0458_force-setup_candidate_801a0458.c spans lines 15-26 (claim cites :15-27); the quoted C matches verbatim.

BAM equivalence note: raw disasm of the pop is `addis r3,r3,1; addi r0,r3,-0x8000` = heading + 0x10000 - 0x8000 = heading + 0x8000, which mod 2^16 is identical to heading - 0x8000; Ghidra's `+ -0x8000` print, the claim's '− 0x8000', and the machine code all agree on the 180-degree flip.

Descriptor stride corroboration: 0x803766f8 - 0x803765e0 = 0x118 exactly, i.e. the two descriptors are adjacent records of stride 0x118 — independent structural support for the claim's 'assuming the two blocks are same-shape' hedge on the 0x118-byte diff.

Mode-byte initiator re-confirmed at the source rather than via the prior doc: zz_0069dd0_ @0x80069dd0 (chunk_0009.c ~:455-475) reads *(char*)(descriptor+0x4b0-load + commandType(+0x585) + 0xb8); == -1 (0xff) returns 0 (command rejected); == 1 routes to zz_0069ea4_ (the 2-D record reader), else falls to the 1-D path — matching the 01/00/ff semantics the borg-switches claim relies on.

verdicts.note: Ctor decomp (organized/game/physics/movement/8019e9a4_...c:6,22-23,29-31) matches all quotes verbatim, incl. Ghidra's '+ 1000' rendering of +0x3e8. My DOL dump @0x80435750 = `00000001 801a0458` then `801a31d8 801a31d8` — byte-for-byte as claimed: groundSlot=0, airSlot=1 (identical to ninja's 0/1), onHit=0x801a0458, trailing words adjacent data not consumed by the engine's proven reads. 0x801a0458 verified as a real function: raw disasm shows standard prologue then `88031d0f lbz r0, 0x1d0f(r3)` — the stream-event gate, same shape as ninja's zz_00715f8_.

Full raw disasm 0x801a0458-0x801a04ec matches instruction-for-instruction. Gate: lbz 0x1d0f/extsb./beq. Pop: lha r3,0x72(r31); addis r3,r3,1; addi r0,r3,-0x8000; sth r0,0x5ae(r31) (net +0x8000 == -0x8000 in 16-bit BAM — claim's reading exact); stfs f3(10.0)->0x44, f2(0.0)->0x4c, f1(15.0)->0x48, f0(-1.0)->0x50; r2-relative operands -0x3504/-0x34e8/-0x3550/-0x3554 against r2=0x8043ea20 (re-derived from raw disasm 0x8000332c-30) resolve to 0x8043b51c=10.0, 0x8043b538=15.0, 0x8043b4d0=0.0, 0x8043b4cc=-1.0 — all four DOL floats re-read and match.
